# curve-apps

Auto-detection of trends and edges in geoscientific data.

```{contents} Table of Contents
:depth: 3
:local: true
```

## Documentation

[Online documentation](https://edge-detection.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)

## Installation

**curve-apps** is currently written and tested for Python 3.10.

### Install Conda

To install **curve-apps**, you need to install **Conda** first.

We recommend to install **Conda** using [miniforge].

### Within a conda environment

You can install (or update) a conda environment with all the requires packages to run **curve-apps**.
To do so you can directly run the **Install_or_Update.bat** file by double left clicking on it.

### Install with conda

You can install the package using `conda` and the `.lock` files from a conda prompt:

```bash
conda env create -n my-env -f environments/[the_desired_env].lock.yml
```

### Install with PyPI

You should not install the package from PyPI, as the app requires conda packages to run.
Still, you can install it in a conda environment without its dependencies (`--no-deps`).

#### From PyPI

To install the **curve-apps** package published on PyPI:

```bash
pip install -U --no-deps curve-apps
```

#### From a Git tag or branch

If the package is not on PiPY yet, you can install it from a Git tag:

```bash
pip install -U --no-deps --force-reinstall https://github.com/MiraGeoscience/curve-apps/archive/refs/tags/TAG.zip
```

Or to install the latest changes available on a given Git branch:

```bash
pip install -U --no-deps --force-reinstall https://github.com/MiraGeoscience/curve-apps/archive/refs/heads/BRANCH.zip
```

:::{note}
The `--force-reinstall` option is used to make sure the updated version
of the sources is installed, and not the cached version, even if the version number
did not change. The `-U` or `--upgrade` option is used to make sure to get the latest version,
on not merely reinstall the same version. As the package is aimed to be in a **Conda environment**, the option `--no-deps` is used to avoid installing the dependencies with pip, as they will be installed with conda.
:::

#### From a local copy of the sources

If you have a git clone of the package sources locally,
you can install **curve-apps** from the local copy of the sources with:

```bash
pip install -U --force-reinstall path/to/project_folder_with_pyproject_toml
```

Or in **editable mode**, so that you can edit the sources and see the effect immediately at runtime:

```bash
pip install -e -U --force-reinstall path/to/project_folder_with_pyproject_toml
```

## Setup for development

To configure the development environment and tools, please see [README-dev.rst].

```
A Grid2D source object.
```

classes.name: curve_apps.edge_detection.params.DetectionParameters
documentation:
Detection parameters expected by the ui.json file format.

```
param line_length

Minimum accepted pixel length of detected lines. (Hough)

param line_gap

Maximum gap between pixels to still form a line. (Hough)

param sigma

Standard deviation of the Gaussian filter. (Canny)

param threshold

Value threshold. (Hough)

param window_size

Size of the window to search for lines.

param merge_length

Minimum length between nodes that should be merged.
```

classes.name: curve_apps.edge_detection.params.Parameters

```
param source

Parameters for the source object and data.
```

classes.name: curve_apps.edge_detection.driver.EdgeDetectionDriver

methods.name: curve_apps.edge_detection.driver.EdgeDetectionDriver.create_output
documentation:
Driver for Grid2D objects for the automated detection of line features.
The application relies on the Canny and Hough transforms from the
Scikit-Image library.

```
param name

Name of the output object.

param parent

Optional parent group.
```

methods.name: curve_apps.edge_detection.driver.EdgeDetectionDriver.get_edges
documentation:
Find edges in gridded data.

:params grid: A Grid2D object.
:params data: Input data.
:params detection: Detection parameters.

:returns : n x 3 array. Vertices of edges.
:returns : n x 2 float array. Cells of edges.

methods.name: curve_apps.edge_detection.driver.EdgeDetectionDriver.get_line_indices
documentation:
Get indices forming lines on a canny image.

The process is done over tiles of square size. The tiles overlap by 25%.

```
param canny_image

Edges.

param line_length

Minimum accepted pixel length of detected lines. (Hough)

param line_gap

Maximum gap between pixels to still form a line. (Hough)

param threshold

Value threshold. (Hough)

param window_size

Size of the window to search for lines.

returns

List of indices.
```

Driver for the edge detection application.

```
param parameters

Application parameters.
```

:params data: Input referenced data.
